style_extra: Part of a matched set of hand-carved Pagade (Pachisi) game pieces in the ancient Indian temple-craft tradition, each a single small piece standing on a slim round carved lotus pedestal base, symmetrical and readable as a game token, museum studio product photo on a seamless warm light-grey background.

items.stupa: A carved temple stupa game piece: a smooth rounded dome (anda) on a stepped square base, crowned by a stacked tapering spire finial with parasol discs (chattravali), serene and sacred, gentle carved mouldings.
items.warrior: A carved ancient Indian warrior game piece: a bare-chested foot-soldier in a knee-length dhoti and a rounded turban-helmet, holding a heavy round-headed mace (gada) upright against one shoulder, standing alert and resolute.
items.lotus: A carved lotus-bloom game piece: a single opening lotus flower of several layered rounded petals rising symmetrically from a small carved calyx and pedestal, elegant and auspicious.
items.kalash: A carved sacred kalash game piece: a rounded bulbous temple water-pot with a narrow neck and flared mouth, topped by a coconut wrapped in five upright mango leaves, ornate ceremonial detail.
items.elephant: A carved caparisoned Indian temple elephant (Gaja) game piece: a small standing elephant with a curled trunk, short tusks, big ears, a decorated ceremonial face-plate and jewelled drape, dignified.
items.pillar: A carved Ashokan pillar (Stambha) game piece: a slender fluted monolithic column with an inverted-lotus bell capital and a round abacus, crowned by the four addorsed Lions of Sarnath sitting back to back, a small Ashoka chakra wheel above.
